title: NOC 52111 – Graphic Arts Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Graphic Arts Technicians (NOC 52111) play a critical role in Canada’s printing and digital media production industry. As businesses increasingly rely on high-quality visual content, packaging, and branding materials, the demand for skilled technicians continues to grow across provinces like Ontario, British Columbia, and Alberta. This occupation offers a practical and accessible pathway to Canada PR, especially through employer-driven programs such as LMIA and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: ClassificationDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER Level2Major Group52 – Technical occupations in art, culture and sportSub-Major Group521 – Technical occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5211 – Technical occupations in motion pictures, broadcasting and the performing artsNOC Code52111

duties: Set up and operate printing and prepress equipment
Prepare layouts, plates, and digital files for printing
Calibrate colors and ensure print quality standards
Monitor printing processes and adjust machine settings
Inspect finished products for defects and consistency
Maintain printing equipment and troubleshoot issues
Coordinate with designers and production teams
employment_requirements: College diploma or certification in graphic arts, printing technology, or related field (preferred)
Experience with printing software and machinery is an asset
job_outlook: Growth in digital and short-run printing
While traditional printing is evolving, technicians with digital and automation skills will have strong long-term prospects.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Graphic Arts Technicians in Canada is driven by:
Growth in digital printing and packaging industries
Increased need for marketing materials and branding
Expansion of e-commerce requiring customized packaging solutions
Ongoing demand in commercial printing, publishing, and advertising sectors
Job opportunities are particularly strong in:
💼 Stable employment in printing, publishing, and media industries
📈 Opportunities to transition into graphic design or production management
🌎 Immigration-friendly occupation under multiple PR pathways
💰 Competitive hourly wages and overtime opportunities
🏭 Work across diverse industries: packaging, advertising, publishing
pathways: Graphic Arts Technicians (NOC 52111) are eligible for several immigration pathways:
Express Entry (CEC / FSW) – depending on experience
Employer-sponsored LMIA Work Permit → PR pathway
